title: Representation Fréchet Loss for Visual Generation
abstract: We show that Fréchet Distance (FD), long considered impractical as a training objective, can in fact be effectively optimized in the representation space. The key idea is to decouple the population size for FD estimation, such as 50k samples, from the batch size for gradient computation, such as 1024. The resulting FD-loss consistently improves visual generators, enables multi-step generators to be repurposed into one-step generators without teacher distillation or adversarial training, and motivates multi-representation FDr^K evaluation because Inception FID can misrank perceptual quality.
publication_status: arXiv preprint; TeX source uses NeurIPS 2026 preprint style, but no formal acceptance or proceedings metadata was verified as of 2026-06-06
